candidates.title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
candidates.summary: Researchers used an OpenAI reasoning model to help diagnose rare diseases, identifying 18 new diagnoses in previously unsolved cases.

candidates.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
candidates.summary: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts of their org, and Meta killed its internal leaderboard. This tension between […]

candidates.title: Amazon SageMaker AI Async Inference now supports inline request payloads
candidates.summary: Today, we’re announcing inline payload support for Amazon SageMaker AI Async Inference. Customers can now send inference payloads directly in the request body of the InvokeEndpointAsync API, removing the need to upload input data to Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) before each invocation.

candidates.title: Get back hours every day with autonomous agents in Amazon Quick
candidates.summary: Today, Quick gets even more powerful: new autonomous agents that work continuously on your behalf, an activity feed that helps you prioritize your most important work, and the ability to find insights across every data source your business runs on from a single question.

candidates.title: Improving health intelligence in ChatGPT
candidates.summary: Learn how GPT-5.5 Instant improves ChatGPT’s health and wellness responses with stronger reasoning, better context, clearer communication, and physician-informed evaluations.
